data.title: As a developer, I can trust the first 26 test files are SIGPIPE-safe
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace all echo|grep -q patterns in the first batch of 26 test files with bash-native equivalents
**Why**: Proves the replacement approach works across all 5 flag variants before scaling to remaining files
**Scope**:
- IN: Files in batch 1 (first 26 files alphabetically from tests/)
- OUT: All other test files (handled by subsequent stories)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, zero echo|grep -q patterns remain in the 26 batch-1 files
  ← Satisfies: "Zero echo ... | grep -q patterns remain in tests/"
- When this story is complete, all tests in the 26 batch-1 files pass
  ← Satisfies: "All replaced patterns preserve original matching semantics"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Each replacement must handle 5 grep flag variants: -q, -qi, -qE, -qiE, -qF
- [Reliability] shopt -s nocasematch for -qi patterns must be scoped (save/restore) to avoid affecting subsequent tests
